title: Merger Arbitrage: The practice of merger arbitrage involves buying and selling shares...
summary: Describe merger arbitrage as buying target shares to profit from expected price changes during M&A.
prompt: The practice of merger arbitrage involves buying and selling shares of the same target company to lock in profits from anticipated price changes during a merger announcement.
solution: Merger arbitrage involves purchasing shares of a target company expecting a price increase due to a merger announcement, alongside selling shares of the acquiring company to hedge against potential losses. The goal is to capitalize on the price discrepancies that often arise during M&A activity. It is not about trading the same asset in different markets, but about strategically positioning to benefit from the merger process.
